children.heading: Persons eligible for program benefits in connection with production or reduced production of crops on certain lands; eligibility based upon compliance with conservation plan by ; minimization of documentationJanuary 1, 1995
children.label: a Persons eligible for program benefits in connection with production or reduced production of crops on certain lands; eligibility based upon compliance with conservation plan by ; minimization of documentationJanuary 1, 1995
A
children.text: cultivated to produce any of the 1981 through 1985 crops of an agricultural commodity; or

children.label: B
children.text: set aside, diverted or otherwise not cultivated under a program administered by the Secretary for any such crops to reduce production of an agricultural commodity.

children.heading: Eligibility based on compliance with conservation plan.—
children.label: 2 Eligibility based on compliance with conservation plan.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: If, as of , or 2 years after the Soil Conservation Service has completed a soil survey for the farm, whichever is later, a person is actively applying a conservation plan, such person shall have until , to comply with the plan without being subject to program ineligibility.January 1, 1990January 1, 1995

children.heading: .—Minimization of documentation
children.label: B .—Minimization of documentation
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary, Soil Conservation Service, and local soil conservation districts shall minimize the quantity of documentation a person must submit to comply with this paragraph.

children.heading: .—Operations new to compliance
children.label: i .—Operations new to compliance
children.text: Notwithstanding , in the case of a person that is subject to for the first time solely due to the amendment made by section 2611(a) of the Agricultural Act of 2014, any person who produces an agricultural commodity on the land that is the basis of the payments described in shall have 5 reinsurance years after the date on which such payments become subject to to develop and comply with an approved conservation plan so as to maintain eligibility for such payments.section 3811(a) of this titlesection 3811 of this titlesection 3811(a)(1)(E) of this titlesection 3811 of this title

children.heading: .—Existing operations with prior violations
children.label: ii .—Existing operations with prior violations
children.text: Notwithstanding , in the case of a person that the Secretary determines would have been in violation of if the person had continued participation in the programs requiring compliance at any time after , and is currently in violation of , the person shall have 2 reinsurance years after the date on which the payments described in become subject to to develop and comply with an approved conservation plan, as determined by the Secretary, so as to maintain eligibility for such payments.section 3811(a) of this titlesection 3811(a) of this titlesection 3811(a) of this titlesection 3811(a)(1)(E) of this titlesection 3811 of this titleFebruary 7, 2014

children.label: I
children.text: only apply to reinsurance years subsequent to the date of a final determination of a violation, including all administrative appeals; and

children.label: II
children.text: not apply to the existing reinsurance year or any reinsurance year prior to the date of the final determination.

Any person who owns or operates highly erodible land that was the subject of a contract entered into under subpart B of part I of subchapter IV shall only be required to apply a conservation plan established under this subchapter. The person shall not be required to meet a higher conservation standard than the standard applied to other highly erodible cropland located within the same area. If the person’s conservation plan requires structures to be constructed, the person shall have until 2 years after the expiration of such contract to comply with the conservation plan, or a longer period of time if the Secretary determines compliance is otherwise technically or economically not feasible, or such longer period is otherwise appropriate, before such person will be subject to program ineligibility with respect to such land under .section 3811 of this title
On the expiration of a contract entered into under subpart B of part I of subchapter IV, the provisions of this subchapter shall apply to the acreage that was the subject of such contract.

children.heading: Persons eligible for program benefits in connection with production of certain planted crops or production of crops on highly erodible land
children.label: b Persons eligible for program benefits in connection with production of certain planted crops or production of crops on highly erodible land

children.text: planted before ; orDecember 23, 1985
planted during any crop year beginning before .December 23, 1985

children.heading: Ineligibility for loans and payments under section 3811
children.label: c Ineligibility for loans and payments under section 3811
A
children.text: within a conservation district, under a conservation system that has been approved by a conservation district after the district has determined that the conservation system is in conformity with technical standards set forth in the Soil Conservation Service technical guide for such district; or

children.label: B
children.text: not within a conservation district, under a conservation system determined by the Secretary to be adequate for the protection of highly erodible land that has been set aside or for the production of such agricultural commodity on any highly erodible land subject to this chapter; or

on highly erodible land that is planted or set aside in reliance on a determination by the Soil Conservation Service that such land was not highly erodible land, except that this paragraph shall not apply to any agricultural commodity that was planted or set aside on any land after the Soil Conservation Service determines that such land is highly erodible land; or

on highly erodible land planted to alfalfa during each of the 1981 through 1985 crop years as part of a rotation practice approved by the Secretary, if the person has submitted a conservation plan, in which case, such person shall have until , to comply with the plan without being subject to program ineligibility under .June 1, 1988section 3811 of this title

children.heading: Program ineligibility inapplicable to pre-, section 3811 loansDecember 23, 1985
children.label: d Program ineligibility inapplicable to pre-, section 3811 loansDecember 23, 1985
children.text: shall not apply to a loan described in made before .Section 3811 of this titlesection 3811 of this titleDecember 23, 1985

children.heading: Limitations on ineligibility for tenants
children.label: e Limitations on ineligibility for tenants
A
children.text: the tenant has made a good faith effort to meet the requirements of this section, including enlisting the assistance of the Secretary to obtain a reasonable conservation plan for such farm; and

children.label: B
children.text: the landlord on the farm refuses to comply with such plan on such farm; and
the Secretary determines that such lack of compliance is not a part of a scheme or device to avoid such compliance.

children.heading: Ineligibility
children.label: 1 Ineligibility
children.text: No person shall become ineligible under for program loans, payments, and benefits as a result of the failure of the person to actively apply a conservation plan, if the Secretary determines that the person has acted in good faith and without an intent to violate this subchapter.section 3811 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: State Executive Director, with the technical concurrence of the State Conservationist; or

children.label: B
children.text: district director, with the technical concurrence of the area conservationist.

children.heading: Period for implementation
children.label: 3 Period for implementation
children.text: A person who meets the requirements of paragraph (1) shall be allowed a reasonable period of time, as determined by the Secretary, but not to exceed 1 year, during which to implement the measures and practices necessary to be considered to be actively applying the conservation plan of the person.

children.label: i
children.text: a person has failed to comply with with respect to highly erodible cropland, and has acted in good faith and without an intent to violate ; orsection 3811 of this titlesection 3811 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: is technical and minor in nature; and

children.label: II
children.text: has a minimal effect on the erosion control purposes of the conservation plan applicable to the land on which the violation has occurred.

children.heading: Reduction
children.label: B Reduction
children.text: If this paragraph applies under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall, in lieu of applying the ineligibility provisions of , reduce program benefits described in that the producer would otherwise be eligible to receive in a crop year by an amount commensurate with the seriousness of the violation, as determined by the Secretary.section 3811 of this titlesection 3811 of this title

children.heading: Subsequent crop years
children.label: 5 Subsequent crop years
children.text: Any person whose benefits are reduced for any crop year under this subsection shall continue to be eligible for all of the benefits described in for any subsequent crop year if, prior to the beginning of the subsequent crop year, the Secretary determines that the person is actively applying a conservation plan according to the schedule specified in the plan.section 3811 of this title

children.heading: Preparation or revision of conservation plan
children.label: g Preparation or revision of conservation plan

children.text: concerning cost effective and applicable erosion control measures that may be available to such individual to meet the requirements of this section; and

concerning crop flexibility, base adjustment, and conservation assistance options that may be available to such individual to meet the requirements of this section, including the provisions of titles X, XII, and XIII of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 (or the amendments made by such titles).

children.heading: Noncommercial production of agricultural commodities
children.label: h Noncommercial production of agricultural commodities
children.text: shall not apply to the noncommercial production of agricultural commodities on a farm if such production is limited to two acres or less and if the Secretary determines that such production is not intended to circumvent the conservation requirements otherwise applicable to lands under this subchapter.Section 3811 of this title
